    Roadmap

    Initiatives

    • Protocol library with LLM-ready metadata
    • Camera monitoring
    • Inventory tracking
    • Voice interface
    • Labware offset tracking
    • Walk-up conversational protocol design
    • User training

    The long-term vision is a walk-up conversational interface for the OpenTrons Flex robot, where researchers describe what they want to do and the system handles protocol design, validation, dry-run confirmation, and execution — hands-free.

    Here’s where each major initiative stands.

    Protocol library with LLM-ready metadata

    Status: Done

    A growing library of working scripts with enough metadata to bundle related examples into future assistant calls (few-shot learning) — an indexed catalog with an extended metadata schema and retrieval built in.

    Next: grow the tested library across more techniques; have the assistant auto-select few-shot examples when writing new protocols.

    Camera monitoring

    Status: In progress

    Tighter communication between the robot and our tools so we can see what’s happening on the deck — still capture and a live stream are working.

    Next: deeper vision interpretation (liquid levels), camera-driven inventory confidence, and a second angle for side monitoring.

    Inventory tracking

    Status: Done

    An independent inventory of labware, tips, and pipettes — on-deck and in storage — tracking contents with confidence values that decay over time.

    Next: auto-update inventory after runs; camera-assisted confidence; broader consumable tracking.

    Voice interface

    Status: Done

    Text-to-speech and speech-to-text so the system can query users verbally, confirm assumptions, and update inventory through conversation.

    Next: hands-free wake word / push-to-talk, streaming TTS, and voice-driven inventory corrections.

    Labware offset tracking

    Status: In progress

    Track calibration offsets so protocols use correct positions, with proactive warnings about uncalibrated labware.

    Next: local caching of calibrated labware and offset status surfaced in deck queries.

    Walk-up conversational protocol design

    Status: In progress

    The full loop: describe an experiment, get clarifying questions, design against the library, validate, dry-run with narration, confirm, run, and update inventory.

    Next: a narrated dry-run mode and an end-to-end test of the complete loop.

    User training

    Status: In progress

    Recognize new versus returning users and offer guided onboarding — vocabulary, the software tools, and a walk-through of a first protocol.

    Next: fuller guided setup and multi-user awareness, eventually recognizing users by face and/or voice.
